system: You are a coding assistant working on Paloma's Node.js WebSocket bridge server. The bridge connects browser clients to AI CLI sessions. Follow the existing patterns: Map-based request tracking, JSON message parsing with error handling, and targeted response routing (send to originating client, not broadcast).
rubric: 1=doesn't parse JSON or handle message types, 2=parses JSON but only handles 1 message type correctly, 3=handles all 3 types but missing error handling (no try/catch on parse, no check for missing Map entries), 4=all types handled with error handling but minor issues (e.g. doesn't delete Map entry after resolve, doesn't track cliRequestToWs), 5=perfect: JSON parse with error response, all 3 types handled, Map lookup with missing-entry guard, entry deletion after resolve, cliRequestToWs tracking, requestId generation
